verses.text: Moses begins to rehearse the law in the plains of Moab. The review. He recounts God's dealings.
Moses begins to rehearse the law in the plains of Moab. The review. He recounts God's dealings.
Moses begins to rehearse the law in the plains of Moab. The review. He recounts God's dealings.
Moses begins to rehearse the law in the plains of Moab. The review. He recounts God's dealings.
Moses begins to rehearse the law in the plains of Moab. The review. He recounts God's dealings.
God bade them leave Horeb and go possess the land. Go in. The promise was set before them.
God bade them leave Horeb and go possess the land. Go in. The promise was set before them.
God bade them leave Horeb and go possess the land. Go in. The promise was set before them.
Moses could not bear all this people alone. Burdened. The load was too heavy for one.
Moses could not bear all this people alone. Burdened. The load was too heavy for one.
Moses could not bear all this people alone. Burdened. The load was too heavy for one.
Moses could not bear all this people alone. Burdened. The load was too heavy for one.
He appointed wise heads over thousands and hundreds. Helpers. The burden was shared.
He appointed wise heads over thousands and hundreds. Helpers. The burden was shared.
He appointed wise heads over thousands and hundreds. Helpers. The burden was shared.
He charged the judges to judge justly, without respect of persons. Justice. Judgment belongs to God.
He charged the judges to judge justly, without respect of persons. Justice. Judgment belongs to God.
He charged the judges to judge justly, without respect of persons. Justice. Judgment belongs to God.
They came to Kadesh; 'Go up and possess... fear not.' The call. The land lay open before them.
They came to Kadesh; 'Go up and possess... fear not.' The call. The land lay open before them.
They came to Kadesh; 'Go up and possess... fear not.' The call. The land lay open before them.
They sent twelve spies, who brought back of the fruit. The spies. They surveyed the good land.
They sent twelve spies, who brought back of the fruit. The spies. They surveyed the good land.
They sent twelve spies, who brought back of the fruit. The spies. They surveyed the good land.
They sent twelve spies, who brought back of the fruit. The spies. They surveyed the good land.
But the people would not go up, murmuring in their tents. Rebellion. Unbelief refused the gift.
But the people would not go up, murmuring in their tents. Rebellion. Unbelief refused the gift.
But the people would not go up, murmuring in their tents. Rebellion. Unbelief refused the gift.
Moses had said, 'the LORD your God... shall fight for you.' Reassured. God had borne them as a father.
Moses had said, 'the LORD your God... shall fight for you.' Reassured. God had borne them as a father.
Moses had said, 'the LORD your God... shall fight for you.' Reassured. God had borne them as a father.
Moses had said, 'the LORD your God... shall fight for you.' Reassured. God had borne them as a father.
Moses had said, 'the LORD your God... shall fight for you.' Reassured. God had borne them as a father.
God swore none of that generation should see the land, save Caleb. The oath. Unbelief lost the promise.
God swore none of that generation should see the land, save Caleb. The oath. Unbelief lost the promise.
God swore none of that generation should see the land, save Caleb. The oath. Unbelief lost the promise.
Even Moses was barred; their children would enter instead. Barred. The cost fell on all.
Even Moses was barred; their children would enter instead. Barred. The cost fell on all.
Even Moses was barred; their children would enter instead. Barred. The cost fell on all.
Even Moses was barred; their children would enter instead. Barred. The cost fell on all.
They presumed to go up against God's word. Presumption. They would not heed the warning.
They presumed to go up against God's word. Presumption. They would not heed the warning.
They presumed to go up against God's word. Presumption. They would not heed the warning.
The Amorites chased them; they wept, but God heard not. Defeat. Too late they would obey.
The Amorites chased them; they wept, but God heard not. Defeat. Too late they would obey.
